zh.lifeSummary: 當Otto I van Holland於大約1135年出生在荷蘭-拉藍德丹 哈格時，他的父親Dirk VI van Holland22歲，母親Sophie von Rheineck22歲。他於1172年之前與Alveradis van Cuijk在荷蘭-拉藍德丹 哈格結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1208年2月13日在德國尼德薩辛格拉福沙福特 本海姆巴德 本海姆享年74歲，埋葬在德國尼德薩辛格拉福沙福特 本海姆維特馬爾陳。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Otto I van Holland于大约1135年出生在荷兰-拉藍德丹 哈格时，他的父亲Dirk VI van Holland22岁，母亲Sophie von Rheineck22岁。他于1172年之前与Alveradis van Cuijk在荷兰-拉藍德丹 哈格结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1208年2月13日在德国尼德薩辛格拉福沙福特 本海姆巴德 本海姆享年74岁，埋葬在德国尼德薩辛格拉福沙福特 本海姆維特馬爾陳。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Holland
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, German, Dutch, Danish, Norwegian, Swedish, French, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from Holland, a province of the Netherlands.

English: habitational name from Downholland or Upholland (Lancashire), Hulland (Derbyshire), the Parts of Holland, one of the three administrative subdivisions of Lincolnshire, any of the four places called Hoyland (southern Yorkshire), and possibly Great and Little Holland (Essex). The placenames all derive from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + land ‘land’.

English: habitational name either from Hoeland (Farm) in Bury (Sussex), or from Holland's Barn in Albourne (Sussex). The placename in Bury has the same etymology as in 1 above, while the placename in Albourne may derive from Old English hol ‘hole, hollow’ + land ‘land’.

variants.name: Hollands
meanings: English: variant of Holland , especially 3, with post-medieval excrescent -s.
Dutch: genitivized form of   Holland  .

variants.name: Howland
meanings: English: variant of   Holland  .
Americanized form of Norwegian   Hovland  .

History: Howland was the name of three Quaker brothers, original settlers in Marshfield, MA. They were from Huntingdonshire, England. The eldest, John Howland (c. 1593–1672) was a passenger on the Mayflower, servant to Gov. John Carver, who died in the first winter at Plymouth Colony.

variants.name: Hollander
meanings: Dutch (also Den Hollander), German (also Holländer), English, Swedish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Holland (see Holland 1).

variants.name: Holand
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Holland  .
Norwegian: habitational name from about twenty farmsteads so named, composed of Old Norse hár ‘high’ + land ‘land, field’, denoting a high-lying farm.

variants.name: Aland
meanings: Scandinavian (Åland): topographic name from the Åland Islands in the Gulf of Bothnia.

English: topographic name from Middle English atte lande (Old French laund) ‘dweller at the glade or pasture’, or sometimes perhaps, ‘dweller at the old cultivated land(s)’ from Old English eald, ald ‘old’ + land ‘land’.

variants.name: Hollon
meanings: English: altered form of   Holland  .

nameMeanings.name: Otto
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a short form of any of the various Germanic compound personal names containing the element od, ot ‘prosperity, wealth’ ( compare the corresponding Old English ēad in names such as Edward and Edwin ). St Otto of Bamberg ( d. 1139 ) was a missionary to the Pomeranians. Otto the Great ( 912–73 ) is generally regarded as the founder of the Holy Roman Empire, and the name has been borne by several members of German and Austrian royal houses. It was recorded occasionally among immigrants to England from the Low Countries in the 14th century, but failed to establish itself. Since the 19th century it has again been used occasionally in the English-speaking world, mostly among immigrants from Germanic countries.

en.lifeSummary: When Otto I van Holland was born about 1135, in The Hague, South Holland, Netherlands, his father, Dirk VI van Holland, was 22 and his mother, Sophie von Rheineck, was 22. He married Alveradis van Cuijk before 1172, in The Hague, South Holland, Netherlands. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 13 February 1208, in Bad Bentheim, Grafschaft Bentheim, Lower Saxony, Germany, at the age of 74, and was buried in Wietmarschen, Grafschaft Bentheim, Lower Saxony, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Otto I van Holland est né environ 1135, à La Haye, Hollande-Méridionale, Pays-Bas, son père, Dirk VI van Holland, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Sophie von Rheineck, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Alveradis van Cuijk avant 1172, à La Haye, Hollande-Méridionale, Pays-Bas. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 13 février 1208, à Bad Bentheim, Grafschaft Bentheim, Niedersachsen, Allemagne, à l’âge de 74 ans, et a été enterré à Wietmarschen, Grafschaft Bentheim, Niedersachsen, Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Otto I van Holland um 1135 in Den Haag, Südholland, Niederlande geboren wurde, war sein Vater Dirk VI van Holland 22 und seine Mutter Sophie von Rheineck 22. Er hat vor 1172 in Den Haag, Südholland, Niederlande Alveradis van Cuijk geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 13. Februar 1208 im Alter von 74 Jahren in Bad Bentheim, Grafschaft Bentheim, Niedersachsen, Deutschland und wurde in Wietmarschen, Grafschaft Bentheim, Niedersachsen, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Otto I van Holland è nato il circa 1135 nel Den Haag, Zuid-Holland, Paesi Bassi, il suo padre, Dirk VI van Holland, aveva 22 anni e il suo madre, Sophie von Rheineck, aveva 22 anni. Lui sposato Alveradis van Cuijk prima del 1172, nel Den Haag, Zuid-Holland, Paesi Bassi. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 13 febbraio 1208, nel Bad Bentheim, Grafschaft Bentheim, Niedersachsen, Germania, all'età di 74 anni, e fu sepolto a Wietmarschen, Grafschaft Bentheim, Niedersachsen, Germania.

ja.lifeSummary: Otto I van Hollandが約1135年にオランダズイド-hオランドデン ハアグで生まれた時，父Dirk VI van Hollandは22歳，母Sophie von Rheineckは22歳であった。1172年以前にオランダズイド-hオランドデン ハアグでAlveradis van Cuijkと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1208年2月13日にドイツニーダーサチセングラフスチャフト ベンスエイムバド ベンスエイムで74歳で死去し，ドイツニーダーサチセングラフスチャフト ベンスエイムウィエトマーシェンにて埋葬された。

ko.Bentheim, Grafschaft Bentheim, Niedersac: 독일 니더작센 그라프샤프트 벤테임 바드 벤테임

ko.lifeSummary: Otto I van Holland(이)가 대략 1135년 네덜란드 주이드-홀랜드 든 하아그에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Dirk VI van Holland(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Sophie von Rheineck(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1172년 이전 네덜란드 주이드-홀랜드 든 하아그에서 Alveradis van Cuijk(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1208년 2월 13일 독일 니더작센 그라프샤프트 벤테임 바드 벤테임에서 74세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 니더작센 그라프샤프트 벤테임 위트마슈에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Otto I van Holland nasceu aproximadamente 1135, em Den Haag, Zuid-Holland, Países Baixos, seu pai, Dirk VI van Holland, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Sophie von Rheineck, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com Alveradis van Cuijk antes 1172, em Den Haag, Zuid-Holland, Países Baixos. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 13 de fevereiro de 1208, em Bad Bentheim, Grafschaft Bentheim, Niedersachsen, Alemanha, com 74 anos, e foi sepultado em Wietmarschen, Grafschaft Bentheim, Niedersachsen, Alemanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Otto I van Holland родился около 1135 г. в местности ден хааг, зуид-холланд, Нидерланды, его отцу по имени Dirk VI van Holland было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Sophie von Rheineck, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Alveradis van Cuijk перед 1172 г. в местности ден хааг, зуид-холланд, Нидерланды. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер 13 февраля 1208 г. в местности бад бентим, графщафт бентим, ниедирсачсен, Германия в возрасте 74 лет и был похоронен в местности виетмарщэн, графщафт бентим, ниедирсачсен, Германия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Otto I van Holland nació aproximadamente 1135, en Den Haag, Zuid-Holland, Países Bajos, su papá, Dirk VI van Holland, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Sophie von Rheineck, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Alveradis van Cuijk antes de 1172, en Den Haag, Zuid-Holland, Países Bajos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 13 de febrero de 1208, en Bad Bentheim, Grafschaft Bentheim, Niedersachsen, Alemania, a la edad de 74 años, y fue sepultado en Wietmarschen, Grafschaft Bentheim, Niedersachsen, Alemania.
